explanation: In a partnership, profit distributions to partners reduce their capital accounts — they are not a salary or expense of the firm. Partners are taxed individually on their share of profit, not on the distribution. This is distinct from an employee bonus.

title: Partner Monthly Drawings (Advance Against Profit)
description: A partner withdraws a monthly drawing against their expected annual profit share.
explanation: Monthly drawings are advances against profit — not salaries. At year-end, total drawings are offset against the partner's profit share. If drawings exceed profit, the partner owes the difference back to the firm. Drawings reduce the partner's capital account.

title: Professional Staff Monthly Payroll (Gross)
description: Recording gross salaries for associates, analysts, and support staff.
explanation: Professional staff salaries are the primary cost of revenue. They are matched to the periods in which services are delivered. Allocation between billable and non-billable time drives the utilization rate — a critical KPI for professional firms.

title: SSC Employer Contribution — Professional Staff (Jordan)
description: Recording the 14.25% employer SSC contribution for Jordanian professional services employees.
explanation: SSC is part of the true employment cost. For a professional services firm, it should be tracked as a separate line within staff costs and allocated between billable and non-billable categories for utilization rate reporting.

title: EOSB Monthly Provision — Professional Staff (Jordan)
description: Accruing the end-of-service gratuity for Jordanian employees of the professional services firm.
explanation: Jordanian labor law requires EOSB provisioning for all permanent staff. For professional firms with high staff turnover (especially associates), careful EOSB tracking prevents unexpected costs when employees depart.

title: Staff Travel Expense — Billable Engagement
description: Recording a consultant's flights and hotel costs for a client site visit to be recharged.
explanation: Billable travel expenses are first recorded as an operating expense when incurred. A separate recharge entry (or combined with the monthly invoice) transfers the cost to the client. The firm's P&L shows the gross expense and the corresponding recovery revenue.

title: Non-Billable Staff Training & CPD
description: Recording CPD courses, exam fees, and technical training that cannot be recharged to clients.
explanation: Continuing Professional Development (CPD) is a mandatory requirement for licensed professionals (lawyers, auditors, engineers). It is expensed as incurred and cannot be capitalized. It is classified as an overhead reducing the firm's net margin.

title: Annual Professional License & Bar/CPA Registration Fee
description: Paying annual renewal fees for professional licenses — bar association, CPA institute, engineering syndicate.
explanation: Professional license fees are mandatory to practice legally. They are an operating expense recognized in the year they relate to. Multi-year licenses are prepaid and amortized monthly.

title: Professional Indemnity Insurance Premium (Prepaid)
description: Paying the annual professional indemnity insurance premium — already in the dataset as a point-in-time entry; this is the separate prepaid recognition.
explanation: Professional indemnity insurance covering 12 months is prepaid and amortized at JOD 400/month. Without current PII, most firms cannot legally practice. It is a fundamental cost of operating in professional services.

title: Professional Indemnity Insurance — Monthly Amortization
description: Releasing one month of the prepaid PII premium to the income statement.
explanation: Each month, JOD 400 of the annual PII premium is recognized as an expense. This matches the cost to the period the coverage protects, regardless of the upfront payment date.

title: Engagement Close-Out — Unbilled WIP Written Off
description: Writing off residual WIP on an engagement that is closed with the client's final invoice already issued.
explanation: At engagement close, any remaining WIP that was not invoiced (because the firm absorbed cost overruns or agreed a fixed scope) is written off. This is a normal part of engagement profitability management.

title: Engagement Close-Out — Final Revenue True-Up
description: Recognizing the final tranche of revenue on a fixed-fee project once all deliverables are confirmed complete.
explanation: At project completion, the final invoice is raised and all remaining contract assets are converted to receivables. Any revenue not yet recognized (deferred under milestones or percentage-of-completion) is recognized at this point.

title: Client Advance Refunded — Engagement Cancelled
description: Refunding an unused client advance when a project is cancelled before meaningful services are delivered.
explanation: If the engagement contract allows for full refund on cancellation with no work done, the entire advance is refunded. The liability is cleared. If some work was performed, revenue for services rendered is recognized first and the remainder is refunded.

title: Referral / Introducer Fee Paid
description: Paying a referral fee to a third party who introduced a new client to the firm.
explanation: Referral fees paid to introducers are a business development expense. They cannot be capitalized as contract acquisition costs under IFRS 15 because they are paid to a third party, not an employee of the firm. Ethical rules for legal and accounting firms must be observed.

title: Referral Fee Received from Another Firm
description: Receiving a referral fee from a law firm or advisor for directing a client to them.
explanation: Referral income is Other Income — it is recognized when the referral qualifies per the agreement (e.g., client signs a contract with the referred firm). Regulatory restrictions on fee-sharing between professionals must be observed.

title: Joint Engagement — Revenue Sharing Between Firms
description: Recording the firm's share of revenue on a joint mandate where two firms collaborate and split fees.
explanation: Where two firms handle a mandate jointly (e.g., legal co-counsel), the lead firm invoices the client for the full amount and remits the other firm's share. Only the lead firm's own revenue share is recognized as income — the co-firm's share is a liability.

title: Staff Secondment — Salary Recharged to Client
description: Recharging the salary cost of a seconded employee placed full-time with a client for a defined period.
explanation: When staff are seconded to a client on a cost-recharge basis, the salary is both an expense of the firm and a recharge to the client. The gross presentation — expense and revenue separately — is required if the firm remains the employer and bears employment risk.

title: Sale of Intellectual Property / Report Licence
description: Selling a licence to use a proprietary methodology, benchmark study, or research report to multiple clients.
explanation: When the firm licences its own IP (databases, proprietary models, published research) to a client for use over a defined period, revenue is recognized at a point in time (for access licences) or over time (for usage-based licences). This is separate from the royalty income entry for third-party IP.

title: Internal Research & Thought Leadership Cost
description: Recording the internal cost of producing proprietary research, whitepapers, and market reports used for business development.
explanation: Internal research that is not commissioned by a specific client is a marketing and business development cost. Under IAS 38, research costs cannot be capitalized. Only development costs meeting six specific criteria may be capitalized as intangible assets.

title: Client Entertainment Expense (Non-Deductible)
description: Recording the cost of a client dinner for relationship development.
explanation: Client entertainment is an operating expense for accounting purposes but is typically disallowed for income tax in Jordan. When computing taxable income, this amount is added back. The firm must document the business purpose and attendees for the expense to be defensible.

title: Network / Franchise Management Fee Paid to Global Firm
description: Recording the monthly fee paid by a local professional services firm to its global network (e.g., Big Four affiliation, legal network).
explanation: Member firms of global professional networks pay affiliation fees (typically a percentage of revenue) for the right to use the brand, share methodologies, and access referrals. This is a recurring overhead expense.

title: Corporate Income Tax Provision — Professional Services Firm
description: Accruing the annual income tax on the firm's taxable profit.
explanation: Professional services entities incorporated as companies pay corporate income tax. Partnerships are typically transparent for tax — partners pay personal income tax on their share. The applicable Jordanian rate depends on the entity type and sector.

title: Annual Research Subscription — Deferred Revenue
description: Collecting an annual subscription fee for access to a proprietary market intelligence platform.
explanation: Annual subscriptions to reports, data platforms, or advisory services are deferred and recognized monthly at JOD 1,000. Revenue is earned as the client has access to the platform — a continuous performance obligation.

title: Research Subscription — Monthly Revenue Release
description: Releasing one month of the deferred annual subscription to earned revenue.
explanation: Monthly release matches revenue to the period in which the subscriber has access to the platform. At year-end, if all 12 months have been released, the deferred revenue balance is zero.

title: Office Lease — Monthly Expense (Professional Firm)
description: Recording the monthly office lease cost for a law firm, consulting firm, or accounting practice.
explanation: Office space is the second largest cost after staff for most professional services firms. Under IFRS 16, longer leases are on-balance sheet. Short leases (under 12 months) or low-value leases may be expensed directly under the exemption.

title: Practice Management / Legal Tech Software Subscription
description: Paying for annual subscriptions to billing software, legal research databases, or document management platforms.
explanation: Annual subscriptions to Westlaw, iManage, Salesforce, or similar practice management tools are prepaid assets amortized at JOD 800/month. These are essential overhead costs for professional services firms.

title: Current Account Opened — Customer Deposit
description: Recording cash deposited by a new retail customer opening a current (demand) account.
explanation: Customer deposits are liabilities for a bank — the bank owes the money back on demand. The cash received increases the vault asset and creates a corresponding deposit liability.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP Banking (FS-CD or FSCM), account opening triggers a CIF (Customer Information File) record; the deposit posts automatically via teller interface generating an FI document. Manual override uses T-Code FB50 if the interface fails. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, the transaction posts through the CASA (Current and Savings Account) module.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors and compliance will flag this entry if the account opening date precedes completion of KYC/AML checks, if the deposit amount is at or above the CBJ cash reporting threshold (JOD 10,000), or if no signed signature card exists on file.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Signed account opening form, valid government-issued ID (both sides), completed KYC/AML checklist, source of funds declaration for deposits above threshold, physical deposit slip or teller receipt, and CIF number assignment confirmation.

title: Savings Account Deposit Received
description: Recording a cash or cheque deposit into an existing retail savings account.
explanation: Savings deposits are interest-bearing demand liabilities. The bank records the inflow as vault cash and increases the deposit liability. Interest accrues separately at the agreed savings rate.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP FS-CD, savings deposit transactions post through the CASA module with product type 'SAV'. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, use the TD/CASA module; the system auto-calculates accrued interest based on the product's interest rate table. Teller journals are auto-generated; manual entry uses T-Code FB50 in SAP FI.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Internal audit checks that interest rates applied match the current product tariff schedule. Regulators flag dormant accounts receiving large deposits. CBJ AML rules require enhanced due diligence for cash deposits exceeding JOD 10,000 in a single transaction.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Completed deposit slip, teller receipt, account passbook entry or e-statement confirmation, and AML screening clearance for amounts above reporting threshold.

title: Fixed-Term Deposit Placed by Customer
description: A customer transfers funds from their current account into a new 3-month fixed deposit.
explanation: A term deposit is reclassified from a demand deposit to a time deposit. For the bank, both are liabilities but with different maturity profiles affecting liquidity reporting. The term deposit typically carries a higher interest rate.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP FSCM/Bank Analyzer, TD placement generates a product object record with maturity date, interest rate, and rollover instructions. In Oracle FLEXCUBE TD module, transaction code 'TDOP' opens the deposit. Ensure the deposit is reflected in ALM (Asset-Liability Management) reports with correct maturity bucket.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors verify that the interest rate applied matches the rate approved by the ALCO (Asset-Liability Committee) for that tenor and amount tier. Early-break penalties must be documented in the product terms. CBJ liquidity coverage ratio (LCR) calculations depend on correct classification of demand vs. term deposits.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Signed term deposit application, deposit advice/confirmation letter, board-approved rate schedule, rollover instruction form, and the originating current account debit confirmation.

title: Interest Accrual on Customer Deposits (Month-End)
description: Accruing interest expense on savings and fixed-term deposits at the month-end.
explanation: Interest on deposits is an expense for the bank. Accrual matches the cost to the period it relates to regardless of when cash is paid. The liability is settled when the deposit matures or on the contractual interest payment date.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP Bank Analyzer or FSCM, interest accrual runs automatically via a batch job (T-Code FINT or equivalent interest accrual program) at month-end. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, the EOD batch 'ACCINIT' calculates and posts interest accruals. Manual override is rare and requires dual authorization.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors reconcile total accrued interest to the system-generated interest trial balance. Variances between the deposit ledger and accrued interest payable trigger investigation. External auditors test the interest rate inputs against CBJ published benchmark rates and board-approved tariff sheets.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: System-generated interest accrual report (by product and account), ALCO-approved rate schedule, month-end interest ledger reconciliation, and sign-off by the Finance Controller.

title: Deposit Interest Paid at Maturity
description: Settling accrued interest when a fixed deposit matures and the customer receives their interest.
explanation: On maturity, the accrued interest liability is settled by either crediting the customer's current account or paying cash. The principal is separately rolled over or returned. This entry clears only the interest portion.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP FSCM, the TD maturity event triggers an automatic posting that settles accrued interest and either credits the linked CASA account or generates a new TD record for rollover. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, 'TDMAT' transaction type handles maturity settlement. WHT deduction (if applicable) is applied at this stage.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Audit checks confirm that WHT (5% in Jordan for individual depositors) was correctly deducted and remitted to ISTD. Any manual override of maturity processing requires dual authorization and is flagged in the exception report.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Maturity advice/confirmation, WHT deduction certificate issued to customer, ISTD WHT remittance schedule, and updated account statement.

title: Premature Fixed Deposit Break — Penalty Applied
description: Customer breaks a 6-month fixed deposit after 2 months; bank applies an early withdrawal penalty.
explanation: Early withdrawal penalties are fee income for the bank. The penalty is typically a reduction in the interest rate applied (e.g., from 4% to 1%) for the actual holding period. The forfeited interest is reversed from accrued interest and recognized as penalty income.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP FSCM, premature closure requires a specific transaction code that triggers an interest recalculation at the penalty rate before settlement. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, 'TDPRE' (premature redemption) handles this with automatic penalty rate lookup from the product definition table.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors verify the penalty rate applied matches the signed TD contract and the bank's published schedule of charges. Waivers of premature penalties require documented approval by authorized management and are reviewed in management override reports.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Signed premature withdrawal request, original TD certificate or digital confirmation, penalty calculation worksheet, revised interest advice, and customer acknowledgment of net amount received.

title: Certificate of Deposit Issued
description: Bank issues a negotiable Certificate of Deposit (CD) to a wholesale investor.
explanation: A CD is a negotiable instrument representing a fixed-term deposit. It is classified as a separate liability from retail deposits on the bank's balance sheet. It typically carries a higher rate due to its wholesale and negotiable nature.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP Bank Analyzer, CDs are managed under the Treasury module as money market instruments with ISIN assignment if listed. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, the MM (Money Market) module handles CD issuance. Bloomberg/Reuters integration may be required for market rate reference on issuance day.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors confirm that the CD is registered in the CD registry maintained by the bank and that duplicate issuances have not occurred. CBJ requires CDs above certain thresholds to be reported in the weekly liquidity return.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Signed CD application, physical or electronic CD certificate with serial number, SWIFT MT199 or equivalent confirmation for wholesale counterparties, CBJ issuance notification (if required), and settlement confirmation.

title: Retail Personal Loan Disbursed
description: Disbursing a personal loan to a retail customer, net of origination fee.
explanation: The loan is recorded at the full gross amount as a financial asset. The origination fee collected upfront is deferred and amortized over the loan life using the Effective Interest Rate (EIR) method under IFRS 9, not recognized as immediate income.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP Loans Management (CML or FS-CML), loan disbursement generates a contract with an amortization schedule. The EIR calculation must be configured in the product master. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, use the CL (Consumer Lending) module; the system auto-calculates EIR at disbursement and schedules fee amortization.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors verify the EIR calculation includes all fees (origination, commitment) per IFRS 9. Credit approval documentation must precede disbursement date. CBJ consumer protection rules require the Annual Percentage Rate (APR) to be disclosed to the customer before disbursement.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Signed loan agreement with full amortization schedule, credit committee approval memo, customer income verification documents (salary certificate, bank statements), disbursement advice, and APR disclosure form signed by customer.

title: Corporate Loan Disbursed
description: Disbursing a term loan to a corporate borrower after drawdown conditions are met.
explanation: The arrangement fee is deferred under IFRS 9 EIR method and amortized over the loan term. The gross loan is the financial asset. Corporate loans often have conditions precedent (CPs) that must be satisfied before drawdown.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP CML (Commercial Mortgage Lending) or FS-CML, the loan contract is linked to a credit facility limit. Drawdown triggers a posting against the facility. In Oracle FLEXCUBE CL module, conditions precedent are tracked as checklist items that must be cleared before the system allows disbursement.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors verify all conditions precedent (CPs) were satisfied and documented before disbursement date. Relationship Manager sign-off, Credit Committee or Board Credit Committee approval (depending on amount), and legal documentation (signed facility letter, security documents) must all predate the disbursement entry.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Signed facility letter, board resolution from borrower, security perfection certificates (mortgage, pledge), conditions precedent checklist sign-off, drawdown request from borrower, and SWIFT payment confirmation.

title: Loan Origination Fee — EIR Setup at Disbursement
description: Establishing the deferred origination fee as part of the amortized cost calculation under IFRS 9.
explanation: Under IFRS 9, the loan is initially measured at fair value plus transaction costs. Origination fees received are deducted from the gross loan to arrive at the net amortized cost carrying amount. The effective interest rate is computed on this net amount.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP Bank Analyzer (Financial Instruments), EIR is automatically recalculated each period using the interest accrual engine. Configure the fee code as 'received at origination' in the product definition so the system includes it in EIR. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, the EIR is stored in the contract and drives the monthly interest income posting.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: External auditors test EIR calculations on a sample of loans. A common finding is that processing fees charged by third parties (not incremental direct costs) are incorrectly included in the EIR calculation. IFRS 9 B5.4.1 lists what qualifies as an incremental cost.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Loan origination fee schedule, EIR calculation workbook signed off by treasury/finance, product definition documentation from core banking system, and auditor's confirmation of EIR methodology.

title: Loan Origination Fee — Monthly EIR Amortization
description: Releasing a portion of the deferred origination fee as interest income each month.
explanation: The origination fee amortizes over the loan's life using the EIR method — typically resulting in a slightly higher effective interest rate than the coupon rate. Monthly amortization increases interest income.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: This posting is fully automated in SAP Bank Analyzer and Oracle FLEXCUBE through the end-of-month interest accrual batch. The amortization follows the EIR schedule generated at origination. Manual entry is only required if the batch fails or a loan is restructured mid-period.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors reconcile the closing deferred fee balance to the origination amount minus cumulative amortization. Accelerated amortization on prepaid loans must be tested. If a loan is restructured, the EIR must be recalculated from the modification date.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: System-generated EIR amortization schedule, month-end interest income ledger reconciliation, and restructuring memo (if applicable).

title: Syndicated Loan — Bank's Participation Share
description: Recording the bank's share of a syndicated term loan where another bank acts as agent.
explanation: In a syndicated loan, each participating bank records its own share as a separate financial asset. The agent bank manages the borrower relationship, but each participant has a direct claim on the borrower for their share.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP CML, syndicated participations are set up as a separate loan contract referencing the syndicate deal ID. In Oracle FLEXCUBE SY (Syndications) module, the participation record links to the agent's facility. SWIFT MT643 messages are typically used for agent-to-participant communications on drawdowns and repayments.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors verify the participation agreement is signed and that the bank's share matches the syndicate allocation schedule. For sub-participation (where the bank has no direct relationship with the borrower), different accounting treatment applies and should be disclosed separately.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Syndicated facility agreement, participation certificate, agent bank drawdown notice, SWIFT settlement confirmation, and credit committee approval for the participation amount.

title: Revolving Credit Facility — Customer Drawdown
description: A corporate customer draws down on an approved revolving credit facility.
explanation: A revolving credit facility allows multiple drawdowns up to an approved limit. Each drawdown increases the loan receivable. Unlike a term loan, the borrower can repay and redraw. The undrawn portion is an off-balance sheet commitment.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP CML, revolving facilities are set up with a limit contract. Each drawdown reduces the available limit and creates a utilization record. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, the CL module tracks the outstanding balance vs. sanctioned limit. Commitment fee accrues on the undrawn portion and is handled separately.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors check that drawdowns do not exceed the approved credit limit. Undrawn commitments must be captured in off-balance sheet schedules and factored into CBJ capital adequacy calculations (credit conversion factors apply). Expired unrenewed facilities used for drawdowns are a major audit finding.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Facility agreement with revolving terms, drawdown request from borrower (SWIFT MT101 or letter), available limit confirmation, and settlement confirmation.

title: Overdraft Facility — Customer Draws Below Zero
description: A business customer's account goes into overdraft within an approved limit.
explanation: When a current account goes negative under an approved overdraft, the negative balance is reclassified to a loan asset for reporting purposes. The customer's account liability balance is reduced (or becomes a receivable). Interest accrues on the drawn amount.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP Banking, overdraft utilization triggers an automatic reclassification from the demand deposit liability to the overdraft loan asset when the account balance crosses zero. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, the OD (Overdraft) product handles this reclassification in the CASA module. Year-end reporting must show overdraft balances as assets, not negative liabilities.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: CBJ regulations require that unauthorized overdrafts (exceeding the approved limit) be immediately escalated and reported. Auditors flag situations where overdraft facilities are repeatedly extended without formal renewal — this suggests the facility has become a disguised term loan.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Signed overdraft facility agreement, approval from credit committee, daily overdraft utilization report, and interest calculation confirmation.

title: Interest Income Accrual — Performing Loan (Stage 1/2)
description: Accruing monthly interest income on a performing corporate loan at the contractual rate.
explanation: Interest on performing loans (Stage 1 and Stage 2 under IFRS 9) is accrued on the gross carrying amount at the EIR. It is recognized as earned regardless of when cash is received, as long as the loan is not credit-impaired.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP Bank Analyzer, interest accrual on performing loans runs via the Financial Instruments (FI) component in the EOD/EOM batch. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, the ACCR (Accruals) batch generates interest entries daily using the product's interest calculation method (30/360, ACT/365, etc.). Day count convention must match the loan agreement.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors test that day count conventions used in the system match those specified in the loan agreement. A frequent finding is that the system uses ACT/360 while the contract specifies ACT/365. For Stage 2 loans, auditors confirm accrual continues on the gross (not net of ECL) balance.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Loan agreement specifying interest rate and day count convention, system-generated interest accrual report, and monthly reconciliation of accrued interest receivable to individual loan balances.

title: Interest Income — Non-Performing Loan (Cash Basis)
description: Recognizing interest on a Stage 3 (credit-impaired) loan only when cash is actually received.
explanation: Under IFRS 9, Stage 3 (credit-impaired) loans accrue interest on the net carrying amount (after ECL provision), not the gross. Many banks default to cash-basis recognition for NPLs to be conservative — interest is only recognized when cash hits the account.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP Bank Analyzer, Stage 3 loans require configuration to switch from gross accrual to net accrual or cash-basis. The Interest in Suspense account is used to capture accrued but uncollected interest separately. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, NPL status triggers a product transfer to a 'non-accrual' product type.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: External auditors scrutinize the threshold for moving from accrual to cash-basis. CBJ regulations specify that interest must be suspended once a loan is 90 days past due. Auditors check that interest in suspense is not included in reported income.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: NPL classification report, CBJ regulatory reporting schedule (Form B), interest in suspense reconciliation, and cash receipt confirmation.

title: Interest Expense — Customer Deposits (Month-End)
description: Accruing interest expense on all deposit accounts at month-end.
explanation: The interest cost on customer deposits is the bank's primary funding expense. It is accrued monthly and settled on payment dates. The net interest margin (NIM) is the difference between interest income on loans and interest expense on deposits.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP Bank Analyzer, deposit interest accrual is part of the same EOD/EOM batch as loan interest accrual but credits the interest payable account. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, the ACCINIT batch processes all CASA and TD accounts. The Finance team reconciles the total against the ALCO rate model each month.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors test that rates applied to deposits match ALCO-approved schedules. Segmentation errors (e.g., applying wholesale rates to retail accounts) are flagged. IFRS 9 requires that deposit interest reflects the EIR including any premium paid to attract deposits.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Month-end deposit interest accrual report by product type, ALCO rate approval minutes, and reconciliation to the interest expense general ledger.

title: Interest Expense — Interbank Borrowing
description: Accruing interest on overnight or short-term funds borrowed from another bank.
explanation: Interbank borrowings are used for short-term liquidity management. Interest accrues at the agreed rate (JOIBOR, LIBOR replacement, or bilateral agreed rate). These are unsecured and carry counterparty credit risk.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP Treasury (TRM), interbank deposits are managed as money market deals with daily accrual. In Oracle FLEXCUBE MM module, 'MMPLACC' and 'MMTAKEON' transactions manage placements and takings respectively. SWIFT MT320 (Fixed Loan/Deposit Confirmation) confirms the deal.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors confirm rates used match SWIFT deal confirmations. Interbank borrowings above CBJ limits require regulatory notification. The balance must reconcile with the counterparty's nostro/vostro statement. Open items older than 3 days in reconciliation are escalated.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: SWIFT MT320 deal confirmation, counterparty bank confirmation, treasury dealing ticket, and nostro/vostro reconciliation report.

title: Interest in Suspense — NPL Transfer
description: Transferring previously accrued interest to a suspense account when a loan becomes non-performing.
explanation: When a loan is classified as non-performing (90 days past due under CBJ rules), previously accrued interest is reversed from income and transferred to an Interest in Suspense account. This prevents overstating income on uncollectible interest.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP Bank Analyzer, NPL reclassification triggers an automatic reversal of accrued interest and posting to the suspense account. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, 'Status Change' event from 'NORM' to 'NPL' drives the interest suspension. This event must be immediately reflected in IFRS 9 staging.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: This is a high-priority audit area. Auditors test the timeliness of NPL classification — late classification understates provisions and overstates income. CBJ examiners specifically test the 90-day rule during on-site inspections. The interest in suspense balance must reconcile to the NPL schedule.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: NPL classification trigger event documentation, credit monitoring report, CBJ regulatory return (Form B), interest in suspense schedule, and RM escalation memo.

title: Expected Credit Loss — Stage 1 (12-Month ECL)
description: Recording the 12-month ECL provision on a performing loan portfolio.
explanation: Stage 1 loans require a 12-month ECL — the loss expected from default events that could occur within the next 12 months. This is calculated using PD (Probability of Default), LGD (Loss Given Default), and EAD (Exposure at Default) models.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP Bank Analyzer (Credit Risk Analyzer), ECL is calculated using the Risk Engine which inputs PD/LGD/EAD parameters from the Risk Models. In Oracle FLEXCUBE with OBP (Oracle Banking Platform), ECL calculations are managed in the OBCL (Oracle Banking Credit Losses) module. Macro-economic overlay adjustments must be approved by senior management before posting.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: External auditors perform model validation on PD/LGD/EAD inputs. CBJ inspectors review whether the ECL model has been independently validated within the past 12 months. Auditors test the completeness of the loan portfolio feeding into the ECL calculation — off-balance-sheet exposures (undrawn commitments, guarantees) must be included.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: ECL model documentation, independent model validation report, ALCO/Board Risk Committee approval minutes, macro-economic overlay justification memo, and reconciliation of ECL balance to loan portfolio.

title: Expected Credit Loss — Stage 2 (Lifetime ECL)
description: Increasing provision to lifetime ECL when a loan experiences significant increase in credit risk.
explanation: Stage 2 transfer (SICR — Significant Increase in Credit Risk) requires a shift from 12-month ECL to lifetime ECL. SICR triggers include: 30+ days past due, covenant breach, rating downgrade, or qualitative indicators of deterioration. The provision jump at stage transfer is typically significant.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP Credit Risk Analyzer, SICR triggers are configured as business rules (days past due threshold, internal rating migration thresholds). In Oracle OBCL, SICR assessment runs as part of the nightly batch and requires manual review and approval for any override of the system's staging recommendation.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Stage 2 migration is the most scrutinized area in bank audits. Auditors test the SICR criteria against CBJ guidance and the bank's own ECL policy. 'Back-testing' of SICR triggers (were Stage 2 loans later written off?) tests model accuracy. Auditors also check for 'staging cliff effects' — large provision jumps not justified by credit evidence.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: SICR criteria policy document, credit monitoring trigger event evidence, IFRS 9 staging override approval (if any), ECL model output before and after staging, and Board Risk Committee review minutes.

title: Expected Credit Loss — Stage 3 (Credit-Impaired)
description: Full impairment provision on a credit-impaired (NPL) loan based on lifetime ECL assessment.
explanation: Stage 3 requires individual assessment for significant loans. LGD is typically based on collateral valuation (haircut applied), recovery estimates from legal proceedings, and time value of money discounting. The provision can be close to 100% for unsecured loans.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP, Stage 3 individual assessments are typically handled in a spreadsheet model linked to SAP via a data upload, as most core banking systems don't support case-by-case collateral waterfall models. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, the 'Manual Provision' feature allows RM input of recovery assumptions approved by the Remedial Management team.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: External auditors perform collateral valuation challenges — they will request independent property valuations and apply their own haircuts. CBJ inspectors benchmark Stage 3 coverage ratios against peer banks. A coverage ratio below 60% on unsecured NPLs will trigger regulatory inquiries.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Individual impairment assessment worksheet, independent collateral valuation (less than 12 months old), Remedial Management approval, legal counsel opinion on recovery prospects, and Board Credit Committee or ALCO approval for provisions above a materiality threshold.

title: ECL Provision Release — Loan Upgraded to Stage 1
description: Releasing excess provision when a previously impaired loan is restructured and reclassified back to performing.
explanation: When a loan is upgraded (SICR reversal or successful restructuring), excess ECL provision is released. This is a credit to the income statement. It must be supported by evidence of sustained improvement — not just one on-time payment.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP Credit Risk Analyzer, the staging event reversal triggers an automatic provision release calculation. In Oracle OBCL, the provision release requires a workflow approval before the accounting entry is generated. The release amount equals the difference between the old and new ECL calculations.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Provision releases are closely scrutinized as they directly increase reported profit. Auditors apply a 'probation period' test — CBJ guidance suggests loans should demonstrate at least 6 months of clean payment history before Stage 2-to-Stage 1 reclassification. Early releases without sustained evidence are reversed.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Restructuring agreement, payment history post-restructuring (minimum 6 months), updated credit assessment, ALCO/BRC approval for the release, and ECL calculation before and after staging.

title: Loan Write-Off — Fully Impaired (100% Provision)
description: Writing off a loan where recovery is deemed remote and the provision covers the full balance.
explanation: Write-off removes the loan and its provision from the balance sheet. It does not change the P&L if fully provisioned. The bank's legal claim against the borrower continues — the write-off is an accounting action, not a forgiveness of debt.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP and Oracle FLEXCUBE, write-offs require multi-level authorization workflows — typically RM, Credit Risk, CFO, and Board approval above certain thresholds. The loan contract is closed in the system but a shadow record must be maintained for recovery tracking. This shadow record is used when 'bank-recovery-written-off-loan' entries are later posted.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Write-offs are a key audit focus. Auditors verify that Board or Board Credit Committee approval was obtained, that legal action has been exhausted or was deemed uneconomical, and that the write-off policy threshold was not circumvented. CBJ requires write-offs above a certain amount to be reported.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Board/BCC approval minutes, legal counsel opinion confirming recovery is remote, remedial management exit memo, CBJ notification (if above reporting threshold), and maintained off-balance-sheet tracking record.

title: Loan Partial Write-Off
description: Writing off the unsecured portion of an NPL while retaining the collateral-covered balance.
explanation: When collateral partially covers the exposure, only the uncovered (unsecured) portion is written off. The remaining balance stays on the balance sheet at the estimated recoverable amount (collateral value less haircut and disposal costs).

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP, partial write-offs require a manual split of the loan contract into a secured and unsecured sub-component before the write-off transaction. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, 'partial write-off' transaction type handles this by reducing the outstanding principal while keeping the collateral linkage intact.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors test the collateral valuation supporting the retained balance. An outdated property valuation (over 12 months old) used to justify the retained balance is a common finding. The haircut applied must reflect current market conditions and expected disposal costs.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Current independent property/collateral valuation, haircut calculation worksheet, Board/BCC partial write-off approval, and updated loan account statement showing remaining balance.

title: Cash Recovery on Previously Written-Off Loan
description: Receiving unexpected payment on a loan that was fully written off in a prior period.
explanation: Recoveries on written-off loans are recognized as income when cash is received. They are presented as Other Income (Loan Recoveries) in the income statement. The original loan asset is not reinstated — only the cash receipt is recorded.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP and Oracle FLEXCUBE, the off-balance-sheet shadow record (maintained after write-off) is the source for posting the recovery. The cash receipt hits the recovery income account directly. The shadow record is reduced by the recovery amount to track total exposure vs. total recovered.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors verify that all cash recoveries on written-off loans are captured in the recovery income line and not netted against current-year provisions. CBJ financial stability reports track system-wide recovery rates on written-off loans.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Cash receipt confirmation, shadow record update, loan recovery income schedule reconciliation, and board notification of significant recoveries.

title: Letter of Credit Issued — Contingent Liability
description: Issuing a documentary letter of credit on behalf of an import customer — off-balance-sheet contingent.
explanation: An LC is a contingent liability until documents are presented and accepted. It is recorded in memorandum/off-balance-sheet accounts. The credit risk exposure is captured in capital adequacy calculations using credit conversion factors (typically 20% for short-term LCs under CBJ/Basel rules).

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP, LC issuance is handled in the Trade Finance module (TRM-TF) generating an off-balance-sheet exposure record. In Oracle FLEXCUBE BC (Bills and Collections) module, 'LISSUE' transaction opens the LC. The LC must also be reflected in the credit limit monitoring system to reduce the customer's available credit line.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors verify completeness of off-balance-sheet disclosures. Unrecorded or expired LCs rolled over without re-approval are a major finding. CBJ Basel III implementation requires banks to include LC exposures in the Leverage Ratio and Capital Adequacy calculations. Auditors also check for UCP 600 compliance in LC terms.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Signed LC application from importer, credit committee approval (or reduction from approved trade line), LC swift message (MT700), credit limit adjustment confirmation, and copy of LC terms approved by compliance.

title: LC Cash Margin Collected from Customer
description: Collecting a 20% cash margin from the importer before issuing the letter of credit.
explanation: Cash margin collected is a liability — the bank holds it as collateral and must return it to the customer if the LC is not drawn. It is not income. It reduces the bank's credit risk on the LC. Margin percentage depends on the customer's credit standing.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP TRM-TF, the cash margin is automatically deducted from the customer's CASA account when the LC is opened and posted to a segregated margin account. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, 'LC Margin' settlement generates the debit to the customer account and credit to the margin liability. The margin is released when the LC expires or documents are settled.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors check that LC margin accounts are reconciled to individual LC records monthly. Unclaimed margins after LC expiry that are not returned to customers represent a liability risk. Over-aged LC margins (held beyond LC expiry) are flagged as dormant liabilities.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: LC application showing margin percentage, customer account debit advice, margin deposit confirmation, and reconciliation of LC margin balance to open LC register.

title: LC Commission and Charges Income
description: Recognizing commission earned for issuing a letter of credit.
explanation: LC commission is typically charged as a percentage of the LC value per quarter or at inception. Under IFRS 15 / IFRS 9, it is recognized over the LC validity period if it relates to the ongoing obligation to honor documents, or at inception if it is a point-in-time service.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP TRM-TF, commission is charged automatically based on the LC product's fee schedule. In Oracle FLEXCUBE BC module, commission accrual and billing is handled by the 'Charge Definition' linked to the LC product. SWIFT MT799 may be used for free-format messages to correspondent banks regarding charges.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors review whether commission is recognized upfront or over the LC tenor. Under IFRS 15, if the commission relates to the bank's contingent performance obligation, it should be deferred and amortized over the LC validity period rather than taken fully at inception.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Approved schedule of charges, commission billing advice, LC validity period documentation, and revenue recognition policy for trade finance commissions.

title: LC Settlement — Documents Presented and Paid
description: Settling the LC by paying the beneficiary (exporter's bank) after compliant documents are received.
explanation: On document acceptance, the contingent liability converts to a real liability/payable. The margin is applied, the balance becomes a customer obligation (loan or account debit), and the nostro account is debited for the payment to the beneficiary's bank.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP TRM-TF, LC settlement triggers a multi-leg posting: margin release, customer balance creation (or CASA debit), and nostro payment. SWIFT MT202 or MT910 confirms the payment to the correspondent. In Oracle FLEXCUBE BC, 'LSETL' transaction handles the full settlement cascade.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors check that document examination was completed within the UCP 600 timeframe (5 banking days) and that any discrepancies were formally accepted by the applicant before payment. Payments made against discrepant documents without customer waiver represent a significant risk and audit finding.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Original LC documents (Bill of Lading, commercial invoice, packing list, certificate of origin), discrepancy notice (if any), customer waiver letter (if discrepancies), SWIFT payment confirmation, and LC settlement advice.

title: Bank Guarantee Issued — Off-Balance-Sheet
description: Issuing a performance or payment guarantee on behalf of a corporate customer.
explanation: A bank guarantee is an unconditional payment commitment to the beneficiary on the customer's behalf. Like an LC, it is off-balance-sheet until called. It carries a 50-100% credit conversion factor for capital adequacy purposes (vs. 20% for short-term LCs).

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP TRM-TF, guarantees are managed similarly to LCs as contingent instruments. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, 'GUARANT' product type in the BC module handles guarantees. The guarantee must reduce the customer's available credit limit in the credit monitoring system.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors verify guarantees are captured in off-balance-sheet disclosures and in capital adequacy calculations. Guarantees issued without formal approval or exceeding credit limits are a serious finding. Called guarantees (where the bank was asked to pay) must trigger an immediate reclassification to on-balance-sheet liability.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Signed guarantee application, credit committee approval, guarantee instrument issued to beneficiary, credit limit reduction confirmation, and register of all outstanding guarantees.

title: Bank Guarantee Commission Income
description: Recognizing quarterly commission earned for maintaining a bank guarantee.
explanation: Guarantee commission is typically charged quarterly in advance as a percentage of the guarantee amount. The portion earned in the period is recognized as income; any unearned portion is deferred. This is the bank's fee for accepting credit risk.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP TRM-TF, guarantee commission accrues automatically per the product's commission schedule. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, the charge definition on the guarantee product handles periodic commission billing. The accrual entry and the billing entry may be separate (accrual vs. invoiced) depending on whether the customer is billed in advance or arrears.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors check revenue recognition against the guarantee validity period. Commissions collected upfront for multi-year guarantees must be deferred and amortized. Point-in-time vs. over-time recognition depends on whether the bank's performance obligation is continuous (availability of the guarantee) or discrete.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Guarantee commission schedule, billing advice to customer, deferred commission calculation (if multi-period), and reconciliation of commission income to outstanding guarantee register.

title: Documentary Collection Fee Income
description: Earning fee income for processing an inward documentary collection (import documents on approval).
explanation: Documentary collections are lower-risk than LCs — the bank acts as an agent, not guarantor. Fees are point-in-time service fees recognized when the service (presenting documents to the importer) is completed.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP TRM-TF and Oracle FLEXCUBE BC, documentary collections ('D/P' Documents against Payment, 'D/A' Documents against Acceptance) are managed as trade finance transactions. Fees are automatically charged on document presentation or acceptance per the schedule of charges.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors verify that documentary collection fees comply with the bank's approved schedule of charges. Waivers of fees for preferred customers must be documented and approved. Collections held beyond normal processing times without client instruction are flagged.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Collection instruction from presenting bank, schedule of charges, fee billing advice, and SWIFT MT410 acknowledgment to presenting bank.

title: FX Spot Transaction — Bank Buys USD from Customer
description: A corporate customer sells USD to the bank at the spot rate; bank receives USD and pays JOD.
explanation: The bank earns the bid-offer spread on FX transactions. The USD received is at the interbank rate; the JOD paid is at the customer rate. The difference is the FX trading income (spread). The USD is recorded in JOD equivalent at the spot rate.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP Treasury (TRM), FX spot deals are entered as FX transactions with value date T+2. In Oracle FLEXCUBE FX module, 'FXSPOT' transaction type handles customer FX. The deal rate must be within the CBJ-approved spread limits. Revaluation of FX positions runs at EOD using the official CBJ rate.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: CBJ closely monitors FX rates offered to customers — they must not deviate from the official rate beyond the permitted spread. Auditors verify that the bank's FX position (long/short USD) complies with CBJ open position limits. Mismatched value dates between the customer leg and the hedging interbank deal are flagged.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: FX deal ticket, customer instruction or dealing confirmation, CBJ rate sheet for the date, nostro account debit/credit confirmation, and treasury position report.

title: FX Spot Transaction — Bank Sells USD to Customer
description: A corporate customer buys USD from the bank at the spot ask rate.
explanation: When selling USD to the customer, the bank charges the ask rate (higher than mid). The spread between mid and ask is trading income. The bank's USD nostro decreases and must be replenished through interbank dealing if at or below the minimum balance.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: Same systems as the buy transaction. In SAP TRM and Oracle FLEXCUBE FX, the bank's FX position is automatically updated. If the position exceeds the CBJ open position limit, the treasury team must execute a cover deal in the interbank market before end of business.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors test that customer rates never breach the CBJ's published spread tolerance. For large transactions, individual deal rates are tested against the bank's dealing room system logs. Unauthorized override of system-generated rates is a serious compliance issue.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: FX deal confirmation, customer instruction, dealing room rate log, CBJ daily rate bulletin, and end-of-day FX position report.

title: FX Forward Contract — Fair Value at Month-End
description: Marking to market an outstanding FX forward contract at the period-end.
explanation: FX forward contracts are derivatives measured at fair value through profit or loss (FVTPL) under IFRS 9 unless designated as a hedging instrument. The fair value is the present value of the difference between the contract rate and the current market forward rate.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP TRM, FX forwards are managed as derivative financial instruments with daily mark-to-market (MTM) valuation. In Oracle FLEXCUBE DV (Derivatives) module, fair value revaluation runs in the EOD batch. Bloomberg or Reuters feeds provide the market forward rates for MTM calculation.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors verify the fair value model used for forwards — typically a DCF on the net settlement amount discounted using the risk-free rate. For hedging relationships (if hedge accounting is applied), auditors test hedge effectiveness documentation and whether criteria under IFRS 9.6.4 are met.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: FX forward deal confirmation (MT300), market forward rate source (Bloomberg/Reuters), MTM calculation workbook, hedge designation documentation (if applicable), and P&L impact approval by treasury controller.

title: Trading Securities — Fair Value Gain (FVTPL)
description: Recognizing an unrealized gain on government bonds held in the trading portfolio.
explanation: Securities held for trading are measured at FVTPL under IFRS 9. All fair value movements — both realized and unrealized — flow through the income statement. For banks, the trading book is a significant source of revenue volatility.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP Bank Analyzer (Financial Instruments), FVTPL securities are revalued daily using clean prices from Bloomberg/Reuters feeds. In Oracle FLEXCUBE SM (Securities Management) module, the daily revaluation batch posts MTM gains/losses. The treasury portfolio must be reconciled daily to the custodian/depository statement.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors verify the price source for fair value — quoted prices in active markets (Level 1) are preferred. For less liquid bonds, Level 2 or Level 3 inputs must be disclosed and subjected to additional audit scrutiny. CBJ requires banks to maintain minimum liquidity buffers in HQLA (High Quality Liquid Assets), which affects trading portfolio composition.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Bloomberg/Reuters price feed printout, portfolio reconciliation to custodian statement, fair value level classification (Level 1/2/3), and daily P&L report approved by treasury controller.

title: FVOCI Securities (Former AFS) — Unrealized Gain in OCI
description: Recognizing a fair value gain on debt securities designated at FVOCI.
explanation: Under IFRS 9, debt instruments that pass the SPPI (Solely Payments of Principal and Interest) test and are held in a 'hold to collect and sell' business model are classified at FVOCI. Fair value changes go to OCI, not P&L, until the instrument is sold. ECL provisions do affect P&L even for FVOCI assets.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP Bank Analyzer, FVOCI classification is set in the Financial Instrument product definition. Revaluation uses market prices and OCI posting is automated. In Oracle FLEXCUBE SM, 'FVOCI' product type handles the bifurcated accounting (coupon income to P&L, fair value change to OCI). ECL on FVOCI assets posts to P&L separately.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors test the SPPI test documentation for each security at inception — cash flows must be solely payments of principal and interest. Business model assessment must be documented and approved by the Board. Reclassification from FVTPL to FVOCI is only permitted in rare circumstances.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: SPPI test documentation per security, business model assessment approved by ALCO/Board, Bloomberg price source, OCI reconciliation, and deferred tax calculation on FVOCI reserve.

title: Held-to-Maturity Bond — EIR Amortization
description: Amortizing the discount/premium on an HTM (amortized cost) government bond using EIR method.
explanation: Bonds classified at amortized cost (old HTM category) are measured using the EIR method. If purchased at a discount, the carrying amount increases to par over the life. The EIR interest income is higher than the coupon rate, with the difference being amortization of the discount.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP Bank Analyzer, the amortized cost calculation is automated via the Financial Instruments (FI) component using the EIR configured at purchase. In Oracle FLEXCUBE SM, the amortization schedule is generated at bond purchase and posts automatically in the EOD batch. Bond position must reconcile daily to the Central Securities Depository (CSD/CBJ) statement.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors verify that the business model for bonds classified at amortized cost is 'hold to collect' — selling before maturity (unless infrequent/insignificant) triggers a tainting rule that forces reclassification of the entire portfolio. CBJ government bonds are typically 100% HQLA and must be tracked separately for LCR purposes.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Bond purchase confirmation, EIR calculation at purchase, CBJ/CSD custodian statement, business model documentation, and any sales during the period with tainting assessment.

title: Repo Transaction — Securities Sold Under Repurchase Agreement
description: Bank sells government bonds with an agreement to repurchase them at a higher price — using securities as collateral for short-term funding.
explanation: A repo is a secured borrowing. The securities are not derecognized — they remain on the balance sheet as the bank retains substantially all risks and rewards. The cash received is a liability (repo payable). Interest accrues at the repo rate on the payable.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP TRM, repos are managed as secured lending/borrowing deals in the Money Market module. The collateral bond is flagged as 'encumbered' in the securities portfolio. In Oracle FLEXCUBE MM, repo deals are entered with the underlying security as collateral. The haircut reduces the loan-to-value of the collateral.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors verify that repos are correctly classified as secured borrowings (not sales). If the counterparty has the right to re-hypothecate the collateral, additional disclosure is required. Auditors also check that the collateral remains on the bank's balance sheet and is properly encumbered/restricted in the securities custody record.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Repo agreement (GMRA standard), deal confirmation, collateral valuation with haircut, encumbrance flag in securities system, and settlement confirmation (typically via Euroclear/CSD).

title: ATM Interchange Fee Income — Other Bank's Customers
description: Earning interchange fee when another bank's cardholder uses the bank's ATM.
explanation: When another bank's customer uses the ATM, the card issuer pays the ATM acquirer (this bank) an interchange fee. This is typically a flat fee per transaction settled through the card network (Visa/Mastercard/JONET). The entries here represent a single transaction; in practice this is batch-processed.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: ATM interchange is typically managed by the card management system (CMS) not the core banking system. Settlement flows through the card scheme (JONET for domestic, Visa/MC for international) and posts to the bank's GL via an automated interface at the end of each settlement cycle (daily or every 2-3 days).
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors verify that interchange rates match the card scheme's published schedule and bilateral agreements. Unreconciled items between the CMS and the GL that are older than 5 business days are flagged. ATM availability rates are a KPI monitored by management.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Card scheme settlement statement, interchange rate schedule, CMS-to-GL reconciliation report, and ATM transaction log.

title: Credit/Debit Card Annual Membership Fee
description: Charging a cardholder the annual card membership fee.
explanation: Annual card fees are recognized over the 12-month membership period, not all at inception. The upfront charge is deferred and released monthly at JOD 5/month. This matches the revenue to the period in which the cardholder has access to the card service.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In card management systems (Temenos, FIS, Mysis), annual fee billing and deferral are handled within the card product definition. The GL interface posts the full fee to deferred income on billing date and releases it monthly. Some banks use simplified straight-line recognition where the fee is recognized evenly over 12 months.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors test the revenue recognition policy against IFRS 15. The performance obligation is continuous access to the card service — revenue should be recognized over time. Recognizing the full annual fee on day one is incorrect under IFRS 15 and is a common finding in smaller banks.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Card product terms and fee schedule, billing date and amount confirmation, deferred fee amortization schedule, and reconciliation of deferred card fee balance to active cardholders.

title: Wire Transfer (SWIFT) Fee Income
description: Charging a customer for an outward SWIFT wire transfer.
explanation: Wire transfer fees are point-in-time service fees recognized when the payment instruction is executed. Under IFRS 15, the performance obligation is fully satisfied when the SWIFT message is sent. Correspondent bank charges may be deducted from the transferred amount (SHA or OUR options).

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In Oracle FLEXCUBE FT (Funds Transfer) module, fees are automatically applied per the product's charge definition when the transfer is processed. In SAP, wire fees are handled by the payment processing module. SWIFT MT103 (customer transfer) or MT202 (bank-to-bank transfer) confirms execution.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors check that fees match the approved schedule of charges. Differential fee rates for different customer segments must be documented and approved. AML compliance requires SWIFT transaction monitoring — alerts on unusual wire patterns are reviewed.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: SWIFT MT103/MT202 confirmation, fee billing advice, customer instruction, beneficiary details confirmation, and AML screening clearance.

title: Account Maintenance Fee (Monthly Service Charge)
description: Charging a monthly service fee for maintaining a corporate current account.
explanation: Monthly account maintenance fees are recognized in the period they are charged. They represent a stand-ready obligation — the bank maintains the account and provides access to payment infrastructure. Recognized on the last day of the month or the billing date.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In Oracle FLEXCUBE, account maintenance charges are applied by the SACCR batch at month-end per the product's charge definition. In SAP Banking, the fee is generated by the account statement run. Fee waivers for premium customers or minimum balance maintenance are configured in the product rules.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors review the fee waiver policy — accounts below a minimum balance should be charged; accounts above the minimum may be fee-free. Systematic failure to charge fees as per product terms reduces fee income and should be investigated. CBJ consumer protection rules require fee transparency.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Account product terms and fees schedule, monthly fee billing report by product, waiver log and approval, and reconciliation to fee income general ledger.

title: Safe Custody / Safety Deposit Box Fee
description: Charging an annual safe custody fee for holding securities or valuables for a customer.
explanation: Annual safe custody fees are deferred and amortized over 12 months — the bank's performance obligation is continuous custody of the assets. The entry mirrors the treatment of annual card fees.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: Safe custody management is typically handled in a dedicated custody management system or a sub-module of the core banking system. The fee billing is automated at the anniversary date. Monthly amortization credits fee income at JOD 10/month for a JOD 120 annual fee.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors verify that safe custody agreements are signed and that the physical inventory of custody items is reconciled annually (vault count). Unclaimed custody items after a long period may need to be reported to the CBJ as dormant assets.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Signed custody agreement, inventory of items held, annual fee billing advice, and physical vault count confirmation.

title: Investment Management / Wealth Advisory Fee
description: Charging a quarterly portfolio management fee to a private banking customer.
explanation: Wealth management fees are typically a percentage of assets under management (AUM) per annum, charged quarterly. The fee accrues over the quarter as the bank provides ongoing portfolio management services.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: Private banking / wealth management fee calculation is usually handled in specialized systems (Avaloq, Temenos WM, Dynamo) that interface with the core banking system for settlement. The fee is calculated on AUM as of the first day of the quarter or the average AUM over the quarter, depending on the mandate.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors verify the AUM calculation methodology matches the investment management agreement. For performance fees, the high-watermark mechanism must be correctly tracked. IFRS 15 requires performance fees to be recognized only when it is highly probable the fee will not be reversed.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Investment management agreement, AUM calculation report, fee billing advice, customer quarterly portfolio statement, and performance fee high-watermark schedule (if applicable).

title: Common Equity Tier 1 Capital — New Share Issuance
description: Issuing new ordinary shares to existing shareholders to strengthen CET1 capital.
explanation: New share issuances increase CET1 capital, improving the bank's capital adequacy ratio (CAR) under Basel III. Share capital is recorded at par; premium above par goes to the share premium reserve. Both are CET1 components under CBJ Basel III framework.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: Share capital transactions are processed in SAP FI via the equity module or Oracle FLEXCUBE's GL. The Companies Control Department (CCD) in Jordan must be notified. The capital increase requires shareholder approval (EGM) and CBJ prior approval before the issuance.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors verify CBJ prior approval was obtained before the share issuance. Companies Control Department registration of the capital increase must be completed within the required timeframe. The use of proceeds (capital injection vs. covering losses) is disclosed in the notes.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: CBJ prior approval letter, EGM resolution, Companies Control Department filing, share subscription agreements, and certificate of increased capital from the CCD.

title: CBJ Mandatory Reserve Requirement Maintained
description: Maintaining the mandatory reserve deposit at the Central Bank of Jordan.
explanation: CBJ requires banks to maintain a minimum reserve balance (a percentage of eligible deposits). This reserve is restricted — it cannot be used for daily operations. It is classified as a restricted cash asset, separate from the operational balance at CBJ.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: The mandatory reserve calculation is performed by the Finance department based on the CBJ's reserve requirement ratio applied to eligible deposits. In SAP/Oracle FLEXCUBE, the CBJ reserve account is a separate GL code from the operational CBJ nostro account, ensuring it is not included in the available liquidity calculation.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: CBJ inspectors test reserve compliance on a daily basis. Breaches of the minimum reserve requirement trigger significant fines. Auditors verify that the mandatory reserve is correctly excluded from the LCR (Liquidity Coverage Ratio) calculation — restricted cash cannot count as HQLA.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: CBJ reserve requirement calculation worksheet, daily reserve balance confirmation to CBJ, monthly reconciliation of reserve balance to CBJ statement, and compliance certificate from the Finance Controller.

title: Transfer to Statutory Loan Loss Reserve (Appropriation)
description: Appropriating a portion of profit to a statutory/regulatory loan loss reserve as required by CBJ.
explanation: CBJ requires banks to maintain a general banking risk reserve (typically 2% of risk-weighted assets or a prescribed minimum). This reserve is appropriated from profit and is part of Tier 2 capital under Basel III. It is not an ECL provision — it is a regulatory equity reserve.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: This is a year-end board-approved appropriation entry posted in the GL by the Finance team. In SAP FI, the journal moves retained earnings to a restricted equity reserve account. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, it is a manual GL entry. The amount is proposed by Finance, approved by the Board, and reviewed by CBJ examiners.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: External auditors verify the reserve meets CBJ's minimum percentage requirements. They check that the reserve is not double-counted as both Tier 2 capital and an ECL provision. The reserve cannot be reversed or distributed as dividends without CBJ approval.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Board resolution on profit appropriation, CBJ reserve requirement calculation, capital adequacy report showing Tier 2 components, and statutory audit sign-off.

title: Dividend Declaration — Board Approved
description: Recording the board's declaration of an annual cash dividend to shareholders.
explanation: Dividend declaration creates a liability once approved by the Board (for interim dividends) or the AGM (for final dividends). In Jordan, bank dividends require CBJ prior approval if they exceed a certain percentage of capital. The payment of the dividend is a separate entry.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP FI, the dividend payable is set up as a separate liability when the Board resolution is recorded. Oracle FLEXCUBE's GL handles this as a manual journal entry. The Investor Relations department manages the shareholder register and payment calculations. WHT (5% or 10% depending on shareholder type) must be withheld at payment.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors verify CBJ approval was obtained before dividend payment. They check that dividends were not paid from revaluation reserves or share premium (only distributable profits). Post-dividend capital adequacy ratio must still meet CBJ minimum requirements.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: CBJ dividend approval letter, Board/AGM resolution, shareholder register, WHT calculation schedule, dividend payment instruction, and post-dividend CAR calculation.

title: Subordinated Debt Issuance (Tier 2 Capital)
description: Issuing subordinated bonds qualifying as Tier 2 regulatory capital under Basel III.
explanation: Subordinated debt is junior to all depositors and senior creditors — bondholders are paid last in insolvency. This makes it qualify as Tier 2 capital under Basel III. Issuance costs are deducted from the carrying amount (EIR method). CBJ prior approval is required.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP TRM, subordinated bonds are set up as debt instruments with a specific Tier 2 classification flag. In Oracle FLEXCUBE, the bond is managed in the MM or Securities module. The amortization of issuance costs follows the EIR method and is automated in the system's interest accrual engine.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: External auditors and CBJ inspectors verify that the instrument genuinely meets Basel III Tier 2 criteria: subordination clause, minimum 5-year original maturity, no early redemption right within 5 years, and no incentive to redeem. Instruments not meeting these criteria are excluded from Tier 2.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: CBJ prior approval letter, board resolution, bond prospectus or term sheet, legal opinion on subordination and Tier 2 qualification, subscription agreements, and capital adequacy impact analysis.

title: Rights Issue — Proceeds Received from Shareholders
description: Receiving payment from existing shareholders exercising their rights to purchase new shares.
explanation: Rights issue proceeds are the same in structure as a primary share issuance. The key difference is that rights are offered proportionally to existing shareholders, giving them the right to maintain their ownership percentage. Both CET1 components are increased.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: Rights issue subscriptions are tracked by the Share Registrar (typically a separate institution in Jordan). Settlement proceeds arrive through the Jordan Clearing and Depository Corporation (CDC). The Finance team posts the capital increase after the subscription period closes and CDC confirms final allocation.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Auditors verify the allotment process was completed within the timeframe specified in the rights issue prospectus. Unsubscribed rights and their treatment (underwriter takeover or cancellation) must be properly accounted for. CBJ and CCD filings must be completed before the capital increase is effective.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: CBJ approval, rights issue prospectus, CDC subscription report, allotment results, share registrar confirmation, CCD filing receipt, and updated share register.

title: Call Money / Notice Deposit Placed (Asset)
description: Bank places short-term funds in call money with another bank overnight.
explanation: Call money is an overnight or short-notice interbank placement used to earn a return on excess liquidity. It is classified as a short-term financial asset (amortized cost). Interest accrues daily at the call money rate.

practitioner_notes.erp_application: In SAP TRM Money Market module, call money placements are entered as MM deals with overnight tenor. In Oracle FLEXCUBE MM module, 'MMPLACC' transaction manages the placement. The deal renews automatically unless terminated by either party.
practitioner_notes.audit_triggers: Call money placements with related parties (affiliated banks) require arm's-length rates and Board approval. CBJ large exposure limits apply to interbank placements. Auditors verify that call money is correctly classified as 'cash and cash equivalents' or 'short-term investments' depending on the call period.
practitioner_notes.required_documentation: Dealing ticket, counterparty bank confirmation, SWIFT MT320 deal confirmation, and treasury position report.

title: Interbank Deposit Placed (Short-Term Lending)
description: Bank places a 1-month deposit with another bank at a fixed rate.
explanation: Interbank placements are short-term financial assets. They carry credit risk from the counterparty bank and are subject to IFRS 9 ECL assessment (usually Stage 1 with a small provision given the short tenor and bank counterparty quality).

practitioner_notes.erp_application: Managed in SAP TRM or Oracle FLEXCUBE MM module. The deal includes counterparty limit check, deal entry, SWIFT MT320 confirmation, and nostro settlement. CBJ interbank exposure reports require daily reporting of all placements above a threshold.
